goal: Land the G5 (Context) parallel-query backbone — UCIL-owned `G5Source` trait + `execute_g5` parallel orchestrator + `assemble_g5_context` PageRank-ranked + session-deduped fusion — with `executor::test_g5_context_assembly` frozen at module root and a pre-baked M1/M2/M3 mutation contract.
plan_summary: Apply the sextuple-validated bundle-coordinated G-backbone template (WO-0070 G3, WO-0083 G4, WO-0085 G7, WO-0089 G8) to G5 (Context). Ship a NEW `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g5.rs` (~700 LOC) carrying the `G5Source` trait, `execute_g5` parallel-fan-out orchestrator with per-source + master deadlines, and a pure-deterministic `assemble_g5_context` function that filters out chunks whose path appears in the CEQP `files_in_context` array (session dedup per master-plan §5.5 line 517) and sorts the survivors by `pagerank_score` descending (master-plan §5.5 line 506 'PageRank, 50x bias'). Defer real production `G5Source` impls (AiderRepoMapG5Source consuming WO-0087's PageRank engine; Context7G5Source / RepomixG5Source wrapping WO-0074's plugin runtimes) to a follow-up production-wiring WO per DEC-0008 §4. Frozen acceptance test at `crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` module root with three local `TestG5Source` behaviours and SA-numbered panic messages. Three-mutation contract (M1 per-source-timeout bypass, M2 session-dedup bypass, M3 ranking-direction inversion) pre-baked in scope_in.
scope_in: 1. CREATE `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g5.rs` — module-coherent single feat commit per DEC-0005, ~600-800 LOC. Carries: module rustdoc citing master-plan §5.5 + §3.2 row 1/7/8/11/20 + DEC-0008 §4 'no-substitute-impls policy' verbatim block (mirroring `g3.rs:42-54` template); `#![allow(clippy::module_name_repetitions)]` crate attr; `use std::collections::BTreeSet; use std::time::Duration; use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};` imports.

2. Public constants in `g5.rs`: `pub const G5_MASTER_DEADLINE: Duration = Duration::from_millis(5_000);` + `pub const G5_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE: Duration = Duration::from_millis(4_500);`. Both `pub` so the frozen test can assert against them. PER-SOURCE DEADLINE MUST be unconditional (NOT `min(master_deadline, ...)`) — see WO-0070 §executor lesson 'per-group `tokio::time::timeout` deadline MUST be strictly larger than tight master deadlines' for the `tokio::time::Timeout::poll` race rationale.

3. Public types in `g5.rs`: `pub struct G5Query { pub query_text: String, pub files_in_context: Vec<String>, pub token_budget: Option<u32> }` (Clone + Debug + Serialize + Deserialize); `pub enum G5SourceKind { AiderRepoMap, Context7Docs, RepomixPack, OpenContextDocs, OpenApiSpec, GraphQlSchema }` (Clone + Copy + Debug + Eq + PartialEq + Serialize + Deserialize); `pub enum G5SourceStatus { Available, Errored, TimedOut }` (mirrors G3SourceStatus); `pub struct G5ContextChunk { pub source_id: String, pub kind: G5SourceKind, pub path: String, pub content: String, pub pagerank_score: f64, pub token_count: Option<u32> }` (Clone + Debug + Serialize + Deserialize); `pub struct G5SourceOutput { pub source_id: String, pub kind: G5SourceKind, pub status: G5SourceStatus, pub elapsed_ms: u64, pub chunks: Vec<G5ContextChunk>, pub error: Option<String> }`; `pub struct G5Outcome { pub results: Vec<G5SourceOutput>, pub master_timed_out: bool, pub wall_elapsed_ms: u64 }`; `pub struct G5AssembledContext { pub chunks: Vec<G5ContextChunk>, pub total_chunks_in: usize, pub deduped_count: usize, pub source_count: usize, pub total_token_count: u32 }` (all Clone + Debug + Serialize + Deserialize as appropriate).

4. Public trait in `g5.rs`: `#[async_trait::async_trait] pub trait G5Source: Send + Sync { fn source_id(&self) -> &str; fn kind(&self) -> G5SourceKind; async fn execute(&self, query: &G5Query) -> G5SourceOutput; }`. The trait is UCIL-owned (DEC-0008 §4 dependency-inversion seam), NOT a re-export of any external wire format — production impls (AiderRepoMapG5Source / Context7G5Source / RepomixG5Source / OpenContextG5Source / OpenApiG5Source / GraphQlG5Source) are deferred to a follow-up production-wiring WO. Cite this verbatim in the module rustdoc, mirroring `g3.rs:42-54`.

5. Public function in `g5.rs`: `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.group.context", skip_all, fields(source_count = sources.len()))] pub async fn execute_g5(sources: &[Box<dyn G5Source>], query: &G5Query, master_deadline: Duration) -> G5Outcome`. Master-plan §15.2 line 1519 prescribes `ucil.group.<name>` span names. Single-line `#[tracing::instrument]` per WO-0085 §planner lesson — DROP numeric casts in `fields(...)` to avoid `clippy::cast_possible_truncation` under `#![deny(warnings)]`; let tracing auto-capture from fn args.

6. `execute_g5` body: (a) record start `Instant`; (b) dispatch each source under `tokio::time::timeout(G5_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE, source.execute(query))` via `futures::future::join_all` OR `tokio::join!`-style fan-out, EACH WRAPPED IN A SEPARATE `tokio::time::timeout` (NOT min'd with master_deadline — see scope_in #2); (c) wrap the entire `join_all(...)` in `tokio::time::timeout(master_deadline, ...)`; (d) on master timeout: synthesise `G5SourceStatus::TimedOut` placeholders in input order so `results[i].source_id == sources[i].source_id()` either way (mirror `g3.rs::execute_g3` lines 280-310 verbatim); (e) compute `wall_elapsed_ms = u64::try_from(start.elapsed().as_millis()).unwrap_or(u64::MAX)` (saturating cast, ~584 million-year overflow safe); (f) return `G5Outcome { results, master_timed_out, wall_elapsed_ms }`.

7. Private helper `join_all_g5` in `g5.rs` mirroring `executor.rs::join_all_g1` (line 1067) and `g3.rs::join_all_g3`: take `Vec<Pin<Box<dyn Future<Output = G5SourceOutput> + Send>>>` and return `Vec<G5SourceOutput>` after polling each via `Pin::new(&mut)`. Annotate with `#[allow(dead_code)]` + rustdoc citing the M1 mutation contract (replacing parallel fan-out with sequential `.await` loop must compile cleanly under `#![deny(warnings)]`) — WO-0070 §executor lesson 'atomic M1 mutation contract via #[allow(dead_code)] on private helper'.

8. Public function in `g5.rs`: `pub fn assemble_g5_context(outcome: G5Outcome, files_in_context: &[String]) -> G5AssembledContext`. Pure-deterministic — NO async, no IO, no `tokio::spawn`, no logging — EXEMPT from §15.2 tracing per WO-0067 §lesson 'pure-deterministic modules: §15.2 tracing does NOT apply'. Body: (a) flatten chunks from `Available`-status results only into `Vec<G5ContextChunk>`; (b) total_chunks_in = flattened.len(); (c) build `BTreeSet<&str>` from `files_in_context` for O(log n) lookup; (d) filter out chunks whose `path` is in the dedup set (session dedup per master-plan §5.5 line 517); (e) sort survivors by `pagerank_score` DESCENDING with stable tiebreak by `(source_id, path)` lexicographic — `chunks.sort_by(|a, b| b.pagerank_score.partial_cmp(&a.pagerank_score).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal).then_with(|| a.source_id.cmp(&b.source_id)).then_with(|| a.path.cmp(&b.path)))`; (f) sum token_counts for `total_token_count`; (g) deduped_count = total_chunks_in - chunks.len(); (h) source_count = distinct source_ids in surviving chunks; (i) return G5AssembledContext.

9. `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs`: ADD `pub mod g5;` declaration in alphabetical order between `pub mod g4;` and `pub mod g7;` (alphabetical-by-module per `lib.rs:11` precedent established in WO-0067 + WO-0068 lessons). ADD `pub use g5::{ assemble_g5_context, execute_g5, G5AssembledContext, G5ContextChunk, G5Outcome, G5Query, G5Source, G5SourceKind, G5SourceOutput, G5SourceStatus, G5_MASTER_DEADLINE, G5_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE };` re-export block in alphabetical order between G4 and G7 re-exports. NOT positional — alphabetical (per WO-0068 §planner lesson resolving the WO-0068 contradiction).

10. CREATE frozen test at `crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` MODULE ROOT (NOT inside `mod tests { ... }`): `#[cfg(test)] #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines, clippy::missing_panics_doc)] #[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 4)] pub async fn test_g5_context_assembly()`. Place the test BELOW `pub async fn test_g4_architecture_query` (~line 3790) and ABOVE `pub async fn test_g8_test_discovery_all_methods` (~line 4511). The test substring-selector `executor::test_g5_context_assembly` MUST resolve uniquely from a `cargo clean` baseline. Cite WO-0068 §planner lesson 'frozen-test selector substring-match REQUIRES module-root placement' in the test rustdoc.

11. Test body — local `TestG5Source` impl (DEC-0008 §4 dependency-inversion seam, mirrors `TestG3Source` at executor.rs:3194). Use `Test` prefix per WO-0085 §executor lesson 'Test-side `Test*` prefix is canonical for in-process trait impls'. Behaviours: `enum TestBehaviour { ReturnChunks(Vec<(String, String, f64)>), Sleep(Duration, Vec<(String, String, f64)>), Error(String), LongSleep(Duration), Empty }` where the tuple is `(path, content, pagerank_score)` per chunk. Build chunks via private `build_chunks` helper that takes (source_id, kind, tuples) and emits `Vec<G5ContextChunk>` with token_count = Some(tuple.1.len() as u32). Implement `G5Source` for `TestG5Source` with all three trait methods (`source_id`, `kind`, `execute`).

12. SA-numbered assertions (8 total, one per scenario) at module root with `(SAn) <semantic name>; left: ..., right: ...` panic-body shape per DEC-0007 + WO-0085 §verifier lesson. Eight scenarios over ~500 LOC: SA1 — basic available case (3 sources, 5 chunks total, dedup against 1 path → assembled.chunks.len() == 4, deduped_count == 1, source_count == 3, results[0].status == Available); SA2 — pagerank ranking descending (chunks emitted with scores [0.1, 0.9, 0.5, 0.3] → assembled.chunks[0].pagerank_score == 0.9, assembled.chunks[1].pagerank_score == 0.5, assembled.chunks[2].pagerank_score == 0.3, assembled.chunks[3].pagerank_score == 0.1); SA3 — session dedup invariant (chunks emitted with paths ["a.rs", "b.rs", "c.rs"], files_in_context = ["b.rs"] → assembled.chunks.iter().all(|c| c.path != "b.rs")); SA4 — per-source timeout (one source sleeps 7 s with G5_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE = 4500 ms → that source's outcome.results entry has status == TimedOut, elapsed_ms in [4500, 5000)); SA5 — error pass-through (one source returns G5SourceStatus::Errored with error.is_some() → assembled drops its chunks, but other sources' chunks survive); SA6 — master deadline trip (master_deadline = 100 ms, all sources sleep 7 s → outcome.master_timed_out == true, outcome.wall_elapsed_ms < 2000 ms, all results TimedOut); SA7 — empty input (zero sources OR all sources return zero chunks → assembled.chunks.is_empty(), total_chunks_in == 0, deduped_count == 0); SA8 — parallelism wall-clock canary (3 sources with 200 ms sleeps each → outcome.wall_elapsed_ms < 500 ms, NOT 600 ms which a sequential .await loop would yield — mirrors WO-0070 SA1 tightened-bound pattern).

13. M1/M2/M3 PRE-BAKED MUTATION CONTRACT — verifier applies each mutation IN-PLACE via `Edit`, runs targeted `cargo test -p ucil-daemon executor::test_g5_context_assembly`, observes the SA-tagged panic, restores via `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/src/g5.rs` (or executor.rs if the test file is mutated), confirms via `md5sum -c /tmp/wo-0091-g5-orig.md5sum`. Snapshot the file BEFORE any mutation. M1 — replace `tokio::time::timeout(G5_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE, source.execute(query))` in `execute_g5` with bare `Ok(source.execute(query).await)` (or equivalent — consult WO-0090 §verifier lesson 'mutation-patch simplification' for minimal-equivalent forms that avoid `unused_imports`). Targeted SA: SA4 (per-source timeout). M2 — replace the `.filter(|c| !dedup_set.contains(c.path.as_str()))` line in `assemble_g5_context` with a no-op (`.filter(|_| true)`) OR comment out the entire line. Targeted SA: SA3 (session dedup invariant). M3 — swap `b.pagerank_score.partial_cmp(&a.pagerank_score)` to `a.pagerank_score.partial_cmp(&b.pagerank_score)` in the `sort_by` comparator (1-character `a`/`b` swap × 2). Targeted SA: SA2 (pagerank ranking descending).

14. Cargo deps: `async-trait.workspace = true` MUST already exist on `crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml` (added in WO-0068; verify via `grep '^async-trait' crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml`). NO new external workspace deps. NO `regex`. NO additional `chrono` / `time` crates beyond what `crates/ucil-daemon` already pulls. If `tracing` is not already in `crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml` deps, add it via `tracing.workspace = true` (it should already be there from prior WOs).

15. Pre-flight word-ban scrub MUST cover production-side files: `grep -niE 'mock|fake|stub' crates/ucil-daemon/src/g5.rs` returns empty. The test-side `TestG5Source` impl in `executor.rs` is exempt under WO-0048 line 363 + WO-0068 lessons §'Test helpers under `#[cfg(test)]` exempt' carve-out — `Test*` prefix differentiates UCIL-internal trait impls (DEC-0008 §4) from external-wire-format mocks (categorically forbidden per CLAUDE.md). Pre-emptive grep: `head -n $(grep -n '^#\[cfg(test)\]' crates/ucil-daemon/src/g5.rs | head -1 | cut -d: -f1) crates/ucil-daemon/src/g5.rs | grep -niE 'mock|fake|stub'` returns empty if g5.rs has zero `#[cfg(test)]` blocks (the test lives in executor.rs).

16. Conventional Commits subject ≤70 chars target (soft). Commit-LOC ~50-line target is soft per `.claude/rules/commit-style.md` — DEC-0005 module-coherence absorbs the single ~700-LOC g5.rs feat commit + the single ~500-LOC executor.rs test feat commit when each is a coherent atomic unit (g5.rs would not compile without the trait + execute_g5 + assemble_g5_context shipping together; the test would not compile without TestG5Source + all 8 SA assertions shipping together). Cite DEC-0005 + WO-0067 ceqp.rs (548 LOC) + WO-0068 cross_group.rs (788 LOC) + WO-0070 g3.rs (721 LOC) + WO-0085 g7.rs (1273 LOC) precedent in commit body.

17. Acceptance commits: estimated 4-5 commits = 1 build (Cargo.toml dep checks if needed) + 1 feat (g5.rs new file + lib.rs mod decl + lib.rs re-export block in ONE cohesive commit per DEC-0005) + 1 test (executor.rs frozen test + TestG5Source impl) + 1 RFR (`ucil-build/work-orders/0091-ready-for-review.md`). Optional 5th commit for AC tightening if any SA bound proves too loose during local cargo test (cite WO-0070 commit `cf24e87` SA1 tightening precedent).

18. RFR (`0091-ready-for-review.md`) MUST carry: (a) `## Disclosed deviations` enumerating any spirit-vs-literal scope_in deviations (e.g., if M1 patch must change shape per WO-0090 §verifier lesson minimal-equivalent forms); (b) `## Production-side .unwrap() / .expect() enumeration` table per WO-0085 §executor lesson — if any `.unwrap_or(u64::MAX)` saturating cast or other `unwrap_or_else` shipped, list with `# Panics`-style justification proving non-panic-path; (c) pre-mutation md5sum snapshot path `/tmp/wo-0091-g5-orig.md5sum`; (d) the M1/M2/M3 mutation table with file/line/patch/SA/restore command per WO-0085 RFR template; (e) AC21/AC22 phase-3 gate disclosure per scope_out #5 (standing protocol).

19. Tracing carve-out disclosure: `assemble_g5_context` is pure-deterministic and DOES NOT carry `#[tracing::instrument]` per WO-0067 §lesson — disclose explicitly in the function rustdoc with verbatim citation of master-plan §15.2 line 1519 + WO-0067 carve-out reasoning ('pure CPU-bound merge functions are exempt from §15.2 tracing'). `execute_g5` IS async/IO/orchestration so DOES carry `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.group.context", skip_all, fields(source_count = sources.len()))]`.

20. Pre-flight verification before push: (a) `cargo build -p ucil-daemon --tests` clean; (b) `cargo clippy -p ucil-daemon --tests -- -D warnings` clean; (c) `cargo test -p ucil-daemon executor::test_g5_context_assembly` PASS in ≤30 s; (d) word-ban grep on production-side files clean; (e) `git log feat/WO-0091-g5-context-parallel-query ^main --merges | wc -l` returns 0 (no merge commits introduced).
scope_out: 1. Real production `G5Source` impls — AiderRepoMapG5Source (would consume WO-0087's PageRank engine), Context7G5Source (would wrap WO-0074's Context7 plugin runtime via JSON-RPC), RepomixG5Source (would wrap WO-0074's Repomix plugin runtime), OpenContextG5Source / OpenApiG5Source / GraphQlG5Source (deferred to future plugin-manifest WOs). Per DEC-0008 §4 dependency-inversion seam pattern, these are deferred to a follow-up production-wiring WO bundling daemon-startup orchestration registration (`McpServer::with_g5_sources` builder + `lifecycle.rs` boot-time wiring), same shape as G3 (WO-0070), G4 (WO-0083), G7 (WO-0085) production-wiring deferrals. EXPLICITLY NOT IN SCOPE for WO-0091.

2. `get_context_for_edit` MCP tool dispatch handler (master-plan §3.2 row 6) — handler wiring into `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs::handle_tools_call` is a follow-up consumer WO. WO-0091 ships the G5 BACKBONE only; the tool-dispatch HANDLER follows the WO-0090 (F10/F15) MCP-tool-dispatch-handler-bundling template separately. NOT IN SCOPE.

3. `understand_code` / `get_conventions` / `get_architecture` / `explain_history` / `generate_docs` / `query_database` MCP tool dispatch handlers (master-plan §3.2 rows 1, 7, 8, 11, 20, 21 — all consume G5) — these tool-dispatch handlers are separate consumer WOs. NOT IN SCOPE.

4. Cross-group RRF fusion of G5 with other groups — already shipped in WO-0068 (`cross_group::execute_cross_group` + `fuse_cross_group`). WO-0091's `execute_g5` returns `G5Outcome` which the cross-group executor consumes via a future `G5Adapter: GroupExecutor` impl; that adapter is a follow-up WO. NOT IN SCOPE.

5. AC21/AC22 (phase-3 gate `scripts/gate-check.sh 3` exit 0) — STANDING PROTOCOL: the executor runs constituent sub-checks (cargo-test, cargo-clippy, mcp-smoke, serena-docker, diagnostics-bridge, ucil-daemon coverage gate via `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov`) in isolation; the verifier re-runs the gate-script entry point from a clean session. Three pre-existing flake escalations carry: `20260507T0357Z-effectiveness-nav-rust-symbol-rs-line-flake.md`, `20260507T1629Z-effectiveness-refactor-rename-python-fixture-missing-symbol.md`, `20260507T1930Z-effectiveness-nav-rust-symbol-doctest-caller-flake.md`. Coverage-gate.sh sccache `RUSTC_WRAPPER` workaround now 44+ WOs deep — substantive coverage measure via `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-daemon --summary-only --json | jq '.data[0].totals.lines.percent'` is the binding floor (target ≥ 80%). NOT-A-BLOCKER.

6. `scripts/reality-check.sh` pre-existing-stash bug — informational only per WO-0090 §verifier lesson. The M1/M2/M3 in-place mutation contract with `git checkout --` + md5sum verify is the authoritative anti-laziness layer. NOT IN SCOPE for repair.

7. `crates/ucil-core` re-exports of `g5::*` types — per WO-0067 deferred-re-export precedent, downstream consumer WOs that wire G5 into the cross-group executor or MCP tool dispatch handlers will import from `ucil_daemon::g5::*` directly. The first MCP-tool-handler consumer WO that wires `understand_code` / `get_context_for_edit` / etc. through G5 SHOULD bundle the `ucil_core` re-exports if any are needed. NOT IN SCOPE for WO-0091.

8. Token-budget enforcement at the assembly layer — `G5Query.token_budget` is captured in the type but `assemble_g5_context` does NOT trim or truncate based on it. Token-budget enforcement is the responsibility of the consumer (the `get_context_for_edit` handler will trim the assembled context to the budget). The `G5AssembledContext.total_token_count` field exposes the unconstrained total so the consumer can decide trimming. NOT IN SCOPE for WO-0091.

9. PageRank score CALCULATION — WO-0091 consumes `pagerank_score: f64` AS A FIELD on `G5ContextChunk` and assumes the calling source supplies it. Real Aider-style PageRank computation is in WO-0087's PageRank engine; Context7 / Repomix sources will assign synthetic scores (e.g., 1.0 / 0.5 / depth-decay) at production-wiring time. NOT IN SCOPE for WO-0091.

10. F10 / F15 production-source-wiring follow-up bundle (carried from WO-0090 net-new follow-up) — real `LspDiagnosticsG7Source` / `EslintG7Source` / `RuffG7Source` / `SemgrepG7Source` impls + `McpServer::with_default_g7_g8_sources()` factory + `lifecycle.rs` boot-time wiring. Eligible for bundling with G5/G3/G4 production-wiring follow-ups in a future omnibus production-wiring WO. NOT IN SCOPE for WO-0091.

11. `review_changes` MCP tool (P3-W11-F11) — depends on F10 (shipped WO-0090) + F11 (P3-W10-F11, shipped). Different architectural axis (consumer of F10's `handle_check_quality` + bonus-context selector). Subsequent solo-feature WO. NOT IN SCOPE.

12. `feedback loop` post-hoc analyser (P3-W11-F12), `warm processors` (P3-W10-F13), and the four phase-3 integration test suites (P3-W9-F11, P3-W11-F13/14/16) — different architectural axes. Eligible for separate WOs. NOT IN SCOPE.
13. Daemon-side `manifest.transport.env` table + spawn-time env propagation (carried from WO-0076 W1+W2; not advanced through WO-0090). NOT IN SCOPE.
14. Doc rot in pre-existing carried-forward comments referring to earlier phases (WO-0085 W4 pattern). NOT IN SCOPE for active scrubbing in this WO; informational only.

15. `cargo metadata` dep-graph claim verification at executor-time — addresses WO-0090 §planner lesson 'scope_out dep-claims must be verified at planner-time'. WO-0091 scope_in #14 verifies via plain `grep` BEFORE depending on async-trait being present; if the grep returns empty, executor adds `async-trait.workspace = true` to `crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml` in the build commit (which itself is OPTIONAL — likely already present from WO-0068 / WO-0070).
16. New `ucil-core` types — G5 ships entirely under `crates/ucil-daemon`. Touching `crates/ucil-core` would risk the `ucil-core` → `ucil-daemon` cycle (per DEC-0008 §4 rationale). NOT IN SCOPE.

17. Modifying existing `tests/integration/*.rs` files — WO-0091's frozen test lives in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` module root, NOT a new integration test file. NOT IN SCOPE for `tests/`-level changes.
18. `ucil-build/feature-list.json` mutation — verifier-only via `flip-feature.sh` post-PASS; executor and planner are forbidden from touching. Standing CLAUDE.md anti-laziness contract.
acceptance: G5Source trait (UCIL-owned, DEC-0008 §4 dependency-inversion seam) ships in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g5.rs`
execute_g5 parallel orchestrator with per-source + master deadlines + master-deadline-trip placeholder synthesis
assemble_g5_context pure-deterministic ranker + session-deduper (PageRank score descending + files_in_context filter)
executor::test_g5_context_assembly frozen at module root with 8 SA-numbered assertions
M1/M2/M3 mutation contract pre-baked: per-source-timeout bypass / dedup bypass / pagerank-direction inversion
lib.rs alphabetical mod decl + alphabetical re-export block (between g4 and g7)
cargo clippy -p ucil-daemon --tests clean under -D warnings
Coverage on ucil-daemon ≥ 80% via env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov
Production-side word-ban scrub (mock/fake/stub) clean on g5.rs
No new merge commits on feat branch (git log feat ^main --merges == 0)
acceptance_criteria: AC01: `test -f crates/ucil-daemon/src/g5.rs` → exit 0
AC02: `grep -q 'pub const G5_MASTER_DEADLINE: Duration = Duration::from_millis(5_000);' crates/ucil-daemon/src/g5.rs` → exit 0
AC03: `grep -q 'pub const G5_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE: Duration = Duration::from_millis(4_500);' crates/ucil-daemon/src/g5.rs` → exit 0
AC04: `grep -q '#\[async_trait::async_trait\]' crates/ucil-daemon/src/g5.rs && grep -q 'pub trait G5Source' crates/ucil-daemon/src/g5.rs` → both exit 0
AC05: `grep -E 'pub (struct|enum) (G5Query|G5SourceKind|G5SourceStatus|G5ContextChunk|G5SourceOutput|G5Outcome|G5AssembledContext)' crates/ucil-daemon/src/g5.rs | wc -l` → 7 (one per public type)
AC06: `grep -q '#\[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil\.group\.context"' crates/ucil-daemon/src/g5.rs` → exit 0
